title: Package Boundaries, Interfaces, Value Ownership, and Lifecycle · Runtime Contract: Interface Placement
summary: Callbacks mutate shared maps, packages import concrete clients and global loggers, byte slices outlive response bodies, and shutdown cannot account for goroutines. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go interface placement increment in directed owned Go crawler graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Define minimal interfaces at use sites and retain concrete constructors and lifecycle ownership at composition boundaries.
Specify ownership and copy rules for URLs, headers, byte slices, DOM selections, records, errors, and report rows crossing boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Interface Placement · Theory Interface Placement
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 1733 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove directed packages, value ownership, interfaces, resources, errors, and lifecycle closure; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define minimal interfaces at use sites and retain concrete constructors and lifecycle ownership at composition boundaries.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-go-interface-placement-1myscp(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_crawler_go_interface_placement_1myscp\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?ownedResources < 14)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?closedResources < 13 \|\| closedResources > ownedResources)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?errorCases < 6)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?leakedOwners > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The decision applies Interface Placement to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Interface Placement.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using minimal and interfaces plus specific case evidence.
